THIRDERA.TokenImageHint: Click to set token image (used on map)

Details.NaturalHealingBonusHint: Extra hit points recovered when using natural healing on a rest (adds to one HP per level and modifiers from feats, items, etc.).

CGS.GrantedSpellsHeadingTitle: From feats, features, equipment, or conditions. A spell may appear here and on your class list—use Cast from this section for SLA-style grants (at-will or uses/day) so grant uses are tracked separately. If a row says Not on sheet, use Add spell to character first. Then full-list: Add to Ready; learned: Prepared count.
CGS.GrantedSpellNotOnActorHint: Add this spell to the character to prepare or cast it from the sheet.
CGS.SpellGrantsItemHint: Drop a spell item onto a row to set it. Optional: uses per day, at-will, caster level (CL), label.
CGS.SpellGrantDropNeedsSpell: Drop a spell item to assign it to this row.
CGS.SpellGrantDropWrongRow: That row is not an editable spell grant.
CGS.SpellGrantCasterLevelTooltip: Caster level (CL) used for this granted spell or SLA.
CGS.AddGrantSpellToActorHint: Create an embedded spell item on this actor so you can prepare, track casts, and use Cast from the sheet (the grant only references the spell by UUID until it is on the sheet).
CGS.AddGrantSpellToActorFailed: Could not add that spell. Check the UUID resolves to a spell document and the console for errors.
CGS.GrantChannelBadgeTooltip: This row is a capability grant (feat, feature, item, etc.). Cast from here for SLA-style grants so uses are tracked on the grant, not your class spell slots.
CGS.GrantChannelBadgeTooltipRtc: This spell appears under Ready to cast from a capability grant (feat, feature, item, etc.), not from your normal class spell list. Cast here; SLA-style grants track uses on the grant channel.
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsAria: Capability-granted spells for this casting class (shown first), separate from class spell slots and preparation.
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsHeadingTitle: Spells listed here come from feats, features, items, or other capability grants—not from your prepared or spontaneous class list.
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsSubheading: These still use this class for DC and caster level where applicable. They are not spells you readied from your class list.
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsGlobalTitle: Capability grants (not tied to a class roster)
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsGlobalAria: Capability-granted spells with no class roster link, separate from class spell slots.
CGS.ReadyToCastCapabilityGrantsGlobalSubhead: These come from equipment, race, or other grants without a class item id. DC and caster level use your spellcasting class when the system needs a class context.
CGS.GrantCastCountHint: Uses today counted for this granted spell or spell-like ability (separate from class slots and the spell item’s general cast count).
CGS.CastFromGrantTooltip: Cast using the capability grant channel (SLA uses/day or at-will), not class spell slots.
CGS.CastMessageFromCapabilityGrant: Cast via capability grant (not class spell slots).
CGS.RtcGrantCastTrackedOnActor: Casts for this at-will or per-day grant are tracked on the actor (capability grant channel), not on the spell item’s Cast field.
CGS.RtcGrantSourcesLineTitle: Items, feats, or features contributing these spells (DC/caster level still use the class shown in the heading).
CGS.TypeOverlaysItemHint: Use this when the rules give the creature extra types or subtypes on top of its normal form—templates, shapechange-style effects, and similar cases. Pick the matching creature type or subtype from the list, or drag in a real compendium or world entry from the sidebar, so the grant is tied to that definition—not a name you type by hand. This does not change an NPC's primary type and subtypes on the Details tab. These rows are additive: the system does not replace primary type or run type-based automation from them yet.
CGS.CreatureTypeOverlayDropHint: Drop a creature type from the sidebar onto this row.
CGS.SubtypeOverlayDropHint: Drop a subtype from the sidebar onto this row.
CGS.MergedTypeOverlaysHint: All sources that add extra types or subtypes are gathered here. For now, read this as additional types alongside the creature's primary type and, for NPCs, the subtypes on Details—nothing here removes or overrides primary type. Type-based automation is not implemented yet.
CGS.ActorTypeOverlaysHint: Record extra types and subtypes for this character in addition to their normal form—templates, GM effects, or tracking shapechange-style cases. Choose from the lists below, using the same compendium or world entries as elsewhere. Primary type and subtypes on the Details tab stay unchanged. These are additive grants; the system does not run type-based rules from them yet.
CGS.TypeOverlayDropWrongRow: Use a creature type row for types and a subtype row for subtypes.

ItemSheet.SenseEditNotAllowed: This item cannot be edited. Unlock the compendium pack if it is locked, or ensure you have owner permission on the document.

THIRDERA.ConditionEffectsHint: Optional modifiers when this condition is active (e.g. AC -2, half speed).
THIRDERA.FeatModifiersHint: Optional modifiers from this feat (e.g. Will save +2, Strength +2). Applied when the character has this feat.
THIRDERA.ItemModifiersHint: Optional modifiers when this item is equipped (e.g. AC +1, Reflex save +2).

SheetCombat.RollInitiativeHint: Uses the active encounter (GM can create one on the current scene). Adds this actor’s tokens if needed, then rolls initiative like the combat tracker.
SheetCombat.RollInitiativeNeedScene: Switch to a scene on the canvas first so an encounter can be created for this world.
SheetCombat.InitiativeAlreadyRolled: Initiative is already set for this actor in the active encounter. Re-roll from the combat tracker if needed.

ApplyDamageHealing.Title: Apply damage / healing
ApplyDamageHealing.NoTargets: Select one or more tokens on the canvas.
ApplyDamageHealing.NoPermission: You do not have permission to modify the selected token(s).
ApplyDamageHealing.SheetButtonHint: Open dialog to apply damage or healing to selected tokens.
ApplyDamageHealing.ToThisTokenHint: Open dialog with this token as the only target.
ApplyDamageHealing.ChatApplyHint: Open Apply damage/healing dialog with this roll's total.

Rest.Title: Take rest
Rest.Intro: Choose what happens when this character rests for the day:
Rest.ButtonHint: Natural healing and optional spell cast / prepared resets.
Rest.NoPermission: You do not have permission to update this actor.
Rest.NaturalHealingAmountHint: Up to {amount} hit points from level, modifiers, and natural healing bonus.
Rest.ResetCastCountsLabel: Reset all spell cast counts to 0
Rest.ResetPreparedCountsLabel: Reset all spell prepared counts to 0
Rest.ChatSummaryResetCgsGrantCasts: Reset capability-grant spell uses for today.

SpellPenetration.InvalidSpellResistance: Spell penetration requires a numeric spell resistance (SR).
SpellPenetration.RollVsTargetHint: Roll spell penetration as the caster vs {name} (SR {sr})
SpellPenetration.RollPickerHint: Choose a creature whose spell resistance applies
SpellPenetration.NoSrTarget: No creature with spell resistance is available to select.
SpellPenetration.NoCasterOrPermission: Could not roll spell penetration (caster missing or no permission).

SpellSave.RollSaveHint: Roll the appropriate save vs. the spell's DC
SpellSave.NoActorToRoll: No character or creature available to roll for.

Concentration.NoSkillItem: This character has no Concentration skill. Add the Concentration skill item, or configure Concentration under modifier-only skills.
Concentration.NoCasterOrPermission: Could not roll Concentration (caster missing or no permission).
Concentration.RollOtherHint: Concentration with custom DC or damage taken while casting
Concentration.OtherSpellLevelNote: Spell level for this cast: {level} (used in the damage-based DC).
Concentration.OtherDamageHint: If set, DC = 10 + damage + spell level. If both damage and custom DC are set, damage is used.
Concentration.OtherCustomDcHint: Use for entangled, weather, or any fixed DC. Ignored if damage is filled.
Concentration.OtherNeedDcOrDamage: Enter damage taken or a custom DC.
Concentration.OtherInvalidDamage: Damage taken must be a number ≥ 0.
Concentration.OtherInvalidCustomDc: Custom DC must be a number.

NPC.RacialBonusHint: Racial or other permanent ability adjustment
NPC.MovementLandHint: 0 = use Speed above (attributes). Set a value to override for the stat block.
NPC.NaturalAttacksEditHint: Add or edit natural attacks on the Stat block tab.

Skills.exclusiveRequiresClass: Exclusive — requires a class that grants this skill
Skills.AcpTooltip: Armor check penalty (from worn armor and encumbrance; the worse of the two applies to skills that use ACP).
Skills.NpcSkillsHintPart1: Embed skills from the sidebar or choose one below. Totals include armor/load
Skills.AddSkillInvalid: That entry is not a valid skill.
Skills.AddSkillFailed: Failed to add skill. See the console (F12) for details.
Skills.NpcClassSkillToggle: When checked, this skill is treated as a class skill on this NPC (row styling and C / CC badge). New skills default to on.
Skills.MiscModifierHint: Flat bonus other than ranks or ability (e.g. racial, synergy, size adjustments you track manually).
Skills.MiscLabelHint: Shown on the skill total tooltip instead of “Misc” (e.g. Large Hide, synergy).

CurrencyWeight.Name: Track Currency Weight
CurrencyWeight.Hint: If enabled, every 50 coins will add 1 lb to the total weight (D&D 3.5 standard).

AuditLogEnabled.Name: Enable Audit Log
AuditLogEnabled.Hint: When enabled, GMs receive whisper notifications in chat when actors or items are created, updated, or deleted.

AuditLogFilterGM.Name: Filter GM Actions
AuditLogFilterGM.Hint: When enabled, actions performed by GMs will not be logged in the audit log.

FirstLevelFullHp.Name: First Level Full Hit Points
FirstLevelFullHp.Hint: When enabled, the first level of the first class added to a character automatically grants maximum hit points from the class's hit die (e.g., d8 = 8 HP). The value remains editable in the level history.

ReimportCompendiumJsonEachLoad.Name: Re-import compendium JSON on each load
ReimportCompendiumJsonEachLoad.Hint: When off (default), system pack JSON is only applied to compendiums that are still empty, which makes world load much faster. Turn on temporarily after a system update if you need to refresh compendium content from the bundled JSON, then turn it off again.

LogClientBootstrapTiming.Name: Log client bootstrap timing (debug)
LogClientBootstrapTiming.Hint: When enabled, logs elapsed milliseconds for each major step of the system ready hook (compendium load, migrations, caches, derived conditions). Client-only; use while profiling slow reloads.

SpellListBrowser.Title: Spell List Browser
SpellListBrowser.SelectClass: Select a class or domain above to view its spell list.
SpellListBrowser.NoSpells: No spells found. For classes, add Level by Class on each spell; for domains, add Level by Domain on each spell.
SpellListBrowser.SelectSpellsFirst: Select one or more spells (check the boxes) to add.
SpellListBrowser.SpellsLeftToSelectForLevel: Spells left to select for this level: {summary}

LevelUp.Title: Level up
LevelUp.SpellcastersFullListMessage: You have access to new spell levels per your class table. Prepare spells on the Spells tab.
LevelUp.SpellcastersLearnedHint: You can add spells known for this level (see class table).
LevelUp.SpellcastersLearnedNextHint: Add spells in the Spell List if desired, then click Next to continue.
LevelUp.SpellsKnownTotalLabel: Total spells known at this level (class table):
LevelUp.OpenSpellListTitle: Open the Spell List to add spells to your character
LevelUp.NewFeaturesNone: No additional class features at this level.
LevelUp.ChooseClassHint: Level up an existing class or add a new class.
LevelUp.NoClassesHint: No classes yet. Add a new class to get started.
LevelUp.RollHpTitle: Roll hit points for this level
LevelUp.SkillPointsMaxRanksNote: Max ranks per skill: class skills = character level + 3; cross-class = half that (rounded down).
LevelUp.SkillPointsHint: Enter points to spend per skill; the resulting rank is shown (→). Class skills: 1 point = 1 rank; cross-class: 2 points = 1 rank.
LevelUp.ResultingRanksTitle: Resulting rank after spending the entered points
LevelUp.SkillModifiersTitle: Ability and misc modifiers (ability = key ability modifier; misc = racial, synergy, or other)
LevelUp.SkillPointsOverBudget: Skill points spent exceed this level's budget. Reduce ranks or leave some points unspent.
LevelUp.SkillPointsOverBudgetBy: Skill points spent exceed this level's budget by {points}.
LevelUp.SkillPointsOverMax: One or more skills have more points assigned than D&D 3.5 normally allows for this level.
LevelUp.SkillPointsOverMaxForSkill: {name} normally tops out at {max} point(s) this level under D&D 3.5 skill rank rules. Higher values are allowed here, but they violate those rules.
LevelUp.SkillPointsRemainingNegativeTitle: Remaining skill points are below zero ({points}).
LevelUp.UnspentSkillPointsMessage: You still have {points} unspent skill point(s) for this level.
LevelUp.UnspentSkillPointsQuestion: Do you want to continue without allocating them?
LevelUp.AvailableFeatsHint: Browse the feats below and open any one to read it before deciding. Use the picker above to choose the feat you want to gain.
LevelUp.GeneralFeatHint: You gain a general feat this level (1st, 3rd, 6th, 9th, etc.).
LevelUp.FighterBonusFeatHint: You gain a Fighter bonus feat this level ({name} level). Only feats marked as Fighter bonus feats are listed.
LevelUp.FeatsNotFighterBonus: Other feats (not eligible as Fighter bonus feat)
LevelUp.NotFighterBonusReason: Not eligible as Fighter bonus feat
LevelUp.NoIncompleteLevels: No incomplete levels for this class.
LevelUp.FeatsNotQualified: Feats you don't qualify for yet
LevelUp.ViewItemTitle: Open item sheet to view details

FeatPrereq.RequiresFeatMissing: Requires feat: {name}. You do not have this feat.

THIRDERA.RemoveClassConfirmWarning: You are about to remove ALL levels of this class from the character. This cannot be undone and will:
THIRDERA.RemoveClassConfirmDetail: Delete the class from the character, remove all level history entries for this class, remove class-granted feats and features, and may remove spells known from this class.

Spells.PreparedHint: Slots filled: how many times you prepared this spell today (out of your daily slots)
Spells.CastHint: Used: how many of those prepared castings you have used
Spells.PreparedCastShortHint: Prepared = slots you filled for this spell; Cast = times you've used it
Spells.KnownOverLimitWarn: This character's {class} spells known for level {level} is over the class limit. Consider removing a spell or adjusting the class table.
Spells.DomainSpellsSubheading: Always prepared; do not count against your normal slots
Spells.ManuallyAddedHint: Spells dropped here that are not on any class list (e.g. custom or from another source). Add Level by Class on the spell sheet to move them to a class list.
Spells.DragDomainHint: Drag a domain here to add it to this class
Spells.ToggleReady: Include in Ready to cast (toggle to add or remove)
Spells.PlaceholderHint: Domain spell not yet on your character. Spells are added automatically when you add the domain.
Spells.NoSpellsKnown: No spells known for this level yet.
Spells.DragFromKnownHint: Drag spells from the Known tab (or click Add to ready there) to track them here.
Spells.ReadyEmptyShortlist: Add spells from the Known tab to track prepared and cast counts here.
Spells.NoSpellcastingClasses: No spellcasting classes found. Add a class with spellcasting enabled to track spells.
Spells.NoSpellcastingClassesWithSpellsHint: You have no spellcasting classes. These spells are not assigned to a class; add a class with spellcasting to prepare and cast them.
Spells.NoSlotsRemaining: No spell slots remaining for this level ({spell}). Cast was still recorded.
Spells.NoPreparedCastRemaining: No prepared cast remaining for {spell}. Cast was still recorded.
Spells.CgsGrantUsesExhausted: No uses remaining today for this granted spell ({spell}), per the feat or feature. Cast was still recorded.
Spells.ResetCastCountsHint: Set all spell cast counts to 0 for this character (e.g. after a full rest, prayer, or study).
Spells.ResetCastCountsNoChange: No spell cast counts to reset.
Spells.ResetPreparedCountsHint: Set all spell prepared counts to 0 (e.g. after rest, before preparing spells again).
Spells.ResetPreparedCountsConfirm: Clear all prepared spell counts? You will need to set prepared counts again in the Known panel.
Spells.ResetPreparedCountsNoChange: No spell prepared counts to reset.
Spells.CannotDeleteClassOrDomainSpell: Cannot remove this spell: it is a domain spell or on your class spell list. Remove the domain or the class first if you want to remove the spell.
